description: A buffer in linear memory is freed on both sides or never freed, corrupting memory or growing it without bound.
body: Failure mode: ownership of a linear-memory buffer crossing the boundary is unclear, so both JS and WASM free it (double-free, corrupting the allocator) or neither frees it (leak), since WASM linear memory is manually managed and JS GC will not reclaim it. Detection signals: allocator corruption or traps after repeated calls; linear-memory size climbing across round-trips; use-after-free reading stale bytes. Do not assume the other side or the GC frees the buffer. Safer fallback: define a single ownership contract (one side allocates and frees, freed exactly once), prefer the binding layer's managed wrappers, and verify with a repeated round-trip leak test that memory stays bounded.
